title: Greedy Search vs. Sampling
concept: Greedy search always picks the absolute highest probability word, leading to repetitive, loop-prone text; sampling adds variety.

exercises.prompt: If an AI uses 'greedy search', what is its strategy for picking the next word?

options.text: Always pick the word with the highest probability score.
Randomly shuffle all words in the dictionary.
Ask a human to type the next word.

exercises.explain: Greedy search always picks the absolute highest probability option, which can lead to repetitive loop issues.

cards.title: Stuck on Repeat!
cards.body: Have you ever noticed an AI repeating the exact same phrase over and over? This happens because it's playing a game of 'always pick the most obvious next word.' Let's find out how we break this cycle!

cards.title: What is Greedy vs. Sampling?
cards.body: Greedy Search always chooses the word with the highest probability score next. Sampling introduces a touch of randomness, choosing words based on a probability distribution so that less likely (but more interesting) words get a chance to be picked.

cards.title: The Buffet Strategy
cards.body: Imagine you are at a giant dessert buffet. A greedy eater will always take the single biggest chocolate cake slice they see, plate after plate, until they get sick of it. A sampling eater rolls a fair dice to decide what to try next—sometimes choosing fruit, sometimes pie, and sometimes cake. This keeps their plate exciting!

cards.title: A Tale of Two Sentences
cards.body:
Given the starting phrase: 'The weather today is...'

• Greedy search might generate: '...good and good and good.' (stuck in a loop)
• Sampling might generate: '...sunny, perfect for a picnic!' (varied and natural)

cards.title: Is 'Best' Always Better?
cards.body: People often think that picking the absolute highest probability word at every single step will make the best overall sentence. In reality, this makes the AI incredibly boring and prone to infinite repetition. A little randomness actually makes the text sound more human!

cards.title: Greedy vs. Sampling
cards.body: Compare how the two methods choose the next word for: 'I love eating...'

right.text: Might pick 'pizza' (90%), but could roll 'tacos' (8%) or 'sushi' (2%).

left.text: Always picks 'pizza' (90% chance). Boring but safe.

exercises.prompt: How does a Greedy Search algorithm choose the next word?

options.text: It always picks the word with the absolute highest probability next.
It rolls a digital dice to pick random words.
It asks the user to pick every word.
It only uses words starting with the letter 'G'.

exercises.explain: Greedy search is deterministic and short-sighted; it only looks at the single highest score for the very next word, ignoring the overall variety of the sentence.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Using sampling makes AI text generation more creative and less repetitive than greedy search.
exercises.explain: Sampling allows the AI to choose less probable words occasionally, which keeps the text creative and prevents repetitive loops.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the explanation of greedy search's biggest flaw.
exercises.sentence: Because greedy search always picks the top word, it often gets stuck repeating itself.
exercises.explain: Without sampling, a greedy algorithm often gets stuck in a loop, repeating the exact same words or sentences.

exercises.prompt:
Analyze these two AI outputs:
Text A: 'The day was very nice and very nice and very nice.'
Text B: 'The day was sparkling with a hint of fresh adventure.'

Which statement is true?

options.text: Text A is definitely using sampling.
Text B is using sampling because it has creative variety.
Both texts are using greedy search.

exercises.explain: Text A is dull and loops on 'very nice'. Text B uses creative, varied words ('sparkling', 'adventure'), which is a classic sign of sampling.

exercises.prompt: Why do we often prefer sampling over greedy search when generating creative stories with an AI?

options.text: Greedy search is faster because it uses a random number generator.
Greedy search gets stuck in repetitive loops; sampling adds random variety to sound natural.
Greedy search always generates random words to surprise the reader.
Sampling never allows the AI to choose the most likely word.

exercises.explain: Greedy search is predictable and gets stuck in loops because it always selects the single highest-probability token. Sampling introduces probability-based choices, yielding much more natural, varied, and creative text.
